commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No automated code repair surfaced; the remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and maintainer clearance of the human-review pause.; Cleared: The diff narrows user-visible exposure of unsupported control-marker text and does not add dependencies, lifecycle hooks, CI changes, credential handling, or new code-execution paths. (sha=8a09f4c1e3232734a08a9bf39a0bcdc36a4e0a79)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared citation-control-marker stripper, applies it to outbound message payload construction and WebChat markdown rendering, adds regression coverage, and updates the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence current-main path: marker-shaped text flows from `buildSendPayloadParts()` into source replies/channel payloads and WebChat Markdown rendering without a citation-specific sanitizer; I did not run a live WebChat or Telegram session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated code repair surfaced; the remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and maintainer clearance of the human-review pause.

target.title: fix(messages): strip unsupported citation markers
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85193.

GPT/OpenAI/Codex web search can emit private citation control markers such as `citeturn2view0` as plain text. Stripping only in the WebChat renderer missed Telegram and other external channel sends because those messages bypass the UI markdown path.

This now strips the unsupported citation-control marker shape in shared outbound send payload construction before text reaches internal WebChat source replies or channel adapters. WebChat markdown sanitization also uses the same helper as a display fallback. Structured citations are unchanged; this path has no citation metadata to render as links.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/citation-control-markers.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts ui/src/ui/markdown.test.ts src/infra/outbound/sanitize-text.test.ts` passed: 4 files, 110 tests
- `git diff --check`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview` clean after the whitespace-preservation fix

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: unsupported GPT/OpenAI/Codex web-search citation control markers leaking into user-visible replies.
Real environment tested: focused outbound message-tool regression coverage for internal WebChat source replies and normal channel adapter sends, plus WebChat markdown sanitizer coverage.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/citation-control-markers.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts ui/src/ui/markdown.test.ts src/infra/outbound/sanitize-text.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: outbound tests assert both internal source replies and normal channel sends remove `turn2view0` before payload/plugin delivery; renderer test keeps WebChat display cleanup on the same helper.
Observed result after fix: raw citation control marker text is removed before it can reach WebChat or external channel text delivery.
What was not tested: live Telegram provider run with real provider-emitted marker text; the common outbound send boundary is covered with a test channel adapter.
target.author: neeravmakwana

commands.author: Takhoffman
